mnemonics.title: STACK OPERATIONS
mnemonics.content: PUSH IT UP, POP IT DOWN - PUSH ADDS TO TOP, POP REMOVES FROM TOP. REMEMBER: LIFO - LAST IN FIRST OUT - LIKE A STACK OF PLATES.

mnemonics.title: QUEUE OPERATIONS
mnemonics.content: ENQUEUE AT REAR, DEQUEUE AT FRONT - ENTER THE LINE AT THE BACK, LEAVE FROM THE FRONT. REMEMBER: FIFO - FIRST IN FIRST OUT - LIKE A TICKET LINE.

mnemonics.title: LINKED ADVANTAGES
mnemonics.content: DYNAMIC - DONT FIX SIZE, EXPAND AS YOU NEED. NO OVERFLOW TILL MEMORY'S DEPLETED.

acronyms.usage: Property of stack where the last element pushed is the first one to be popped
Property of queue where the first element enqueued is the first one to be dequeued
Indicates empty stack/queue or end of linked list
Pointer variable that points to the top node in a linked stack
Pointers in linked queue - FRONT for deletion, REAR for insertion

visualTips.concept: Linked Stack as a Vertical Chain
visualTips.visualization: Imagine a vertical chain of plates hanging from a ceiling. The TOP is the bottom-most plate. PUSH adds a plate at bottom, POP removes from bottom. Each plate knows only about the plate above it.

visualTips.concept: Linked Queue as a People Line
visualTips.visualization: Think of people standing in a ticket line. NEW people join at the BACK (REAR). People leave from the FRONT. The line can grow indefinitely backward. Each person knows only the person behind them.
Draw a box divided into two parts: left for DATA, right for POINTER (arrow). The arrow points to the next node box, forming a connected chain.

analogies.analogy: Like a stack of books on a desk. You can only add or remove from the top. You don't need to know how many books can fit - you just keep stacking.
Like cars in a drive-through. Cars enter at the back and leave from the front. The line can be as long as needed - limited only by physical space, not a fixed array.
Like requesting tables at a restaurant. Instead of reserving 100 tables upfront whether needed or not, you ask for a table only when a customer arrives, and return it when they leave.
Like an empty parking space or a 'closed' sign. When TOP is NULL, there's nothing in the stack - like an empty parking lot.
